jemdoc-cvx – download

jemdoc-cvx is distributed as source on GitHub: 

git clone https://github.com/cvxgrp/jemdoc-cvx.git

The single executable script is ./jemdoc at the repo root. It uses only the Python standard library, so any Python 3.11+ interpreter is enough to run it. You can put it on your PATH directly: 

sudo install ./jemdoc /usr/local/bin/jemdoc

For server-side equation rendering, also install Node.js and the pinned KaTeX CLI from this repo: 

npm install

If katex is not on PATH, jemdoc falls back to emitting raw \(..\) / \..\ delimiters that a client-side script (e.g. MathJax loaded via a conf override) can render in the browser.

CSS

Stylesheets live in css/ in the repo. jemdoc.css is the default; jacob.css, white.css, green.css, exp.css, and a few others are provided as alternative themes. Copy whichever one you want next to your output html, or load it from a conf file via the [defaultcss] block.

Where to next?

Read about how to use jemdoc, check out an example page, then refer to the cheat sheet once you're underway. You can also study the source of this index page as a starting point.
